headline: Homeschooling Registration Triples to 18,000 Students Nationally
context: Registered homeschooled students tripled from 6,000 in 2020 to 18,000 in 2025 with regulatory gaps widening.

cards.big: 18,000 registered homeschool students in 2025 tripled from 6,000 in 2020
cards.sub: Actual numbers may be higher as registration is not legally mandatory

cards.big: Education Act 1996 Section 29A makes school attendance compulsory but enforcement is minimal
cards.sub: No government agency systematically monitors homeschool learning outcomes

cards.big: 72% of homeschooling families cite dissatisfaction with national school quality
cards.sub: Bullying exam pressure and rigid curriculum are top 3 reasons for withdrawal

cards.big: UK requires local councils to verify homeschool children receive suitable education
cards.sub: Light-touch oversight balances parental freedom with child welfare protection

cards.big: When 18,000 families opt out of public schools the system should ask why not punish them
Should homeschooled children be required to take national assessments at key milestones
